sciopy

sciopy

ScioPy Documentation!

Contents:

  • Install

Functions:

DisableLED_AutoMode(serial)

Disable automode of all LEDs.

EnableLED_AutoMode(serial)

Enable automode of all LEDs.

GetBatteryControll(serial)

Get batterie controll information.

GetDevideInfo(serial)

Print device information.

GetFirmwareIDs(serial)

Print devide firmware.

GetLEDControl(serial, led, mode)

Set a defined led to mode off, on or blink.

GetMeasurementSetup(serial)

Print information about the current configuration.

GetTemperature(serial)

Get temperature value.

PowerPlugDetect(serial)

Print power plug detect information.

ResetMeasurementSetup(serial)

Reset the measurement setup.

SaveSettings(serial)

Save the settings inside the ScioSpec device.

SetBatteryControll(serial)

Set batterie controll.

SetBurstCount(serial, measuremen_cnf)

Set the burst count to one of the provided values [1, 5, 10, 100].

SetLEDControl(serial, led, mode)

Disable or enable auto mode.

SetLED_Mode(serial, led, mode)

Set a defined led to a mode.

SetMeasurementSetup(serial[, burst_count, ...])

Configurate and write own measurement setup.

SoftwareReset(serial)

Reset the ScioSpec software.

StartStopMeasurement(serial)

Start and stop the measurement and return the serial message buffer.

SystemMessageCallback(serial[, prnt_msg, ...])

Reads the message buffer of a serial connection.

available_serial_ports()

Lists serial port names.

bytesarray_to_byteslist(bytes_array)

Converts a bytes array to a list of bytes.

bytesarray_to_float(bytes_array)

Converts a bytes array to a float number.

bytesarray_to_int(bytes_array)

Converts a bytes array to int number.

configuration_01(serial, cnf)

Standart SioSpce configuration template.

configuration_02(serial, cnf)

Amplitude [mA] 1.0 Range [V] +/-5 Framerate 1 Gain 1 Burst Count 5

configuration_03(serial, cnf)

Amplitude [mA] 1.0 Range [V] +/-1 Framerate 1 Gain 1 Burst Count 5

configuration_04(serial, cnf)

Amplitude [mA] 1.0 Range [V] +/-5 Framerate 5 Gain 1 Burst Count 100

configuration_05(serial, cnf)

Amplitude [mA] 1.0 Range [V] +/-10 Framerate 5 Gain 1 Burst Count 10

configure_configuration(serial, cnf)

TBD

connect_COM_port([port, baudrate, timeout])

Etablishes a serial connection to a com port.

convert_fulldir_doteit_to_npz(lpath, spath)

Converts all .eit files in a directory to .npz files in a directory spath.

convert_fulldir_doteit_to_pickle(lpath, spath)

Converts all .eit files in a directory to .pickle files in a directory spath.

del_hex_in_list(lst)

Delete the hexadecimal 0x python notation.

disconnect_COM_port(ser)

Disconnect serial connection.

doteit_in_SingleEitFrame(read_content)

Returns single object without saving anything.

list_all_files(path)

Print a list of all files inside a directory.

list_eit_files(path)

Returns a list of all .eit files in the directory path.

load_pickle_to_dict(path)

Load a pickle file into a python dictionary.

parse_single_frame(lst_ele)

Parse single data to the class SingleFrame.

print_acknowledge_messages()

Print information regarding the acknowledge messages.

print_command_list()

Print the general command list.

print_general_system_messages()

Print information regarding the system messages.

print_syntax()

Print information regarding the communication.

reduce_burst_to_available_parts(brst_cnt[, ...])

Reduces a given burst count to a set of numbers in a defined burst count border list.

reduce_burst_to_less_x(brst_cnt[, leq])

Reduces a given burst count to a list of numbers smaller than a defined border.

reshape_full_message_in_bursts(lst, cnf)

Takes the full message buffer and splits this message depeding on the measurement configuration into the burst count parts.

serial_write(ser, cmd)

Write a string to a serial connection.

single_eit_in_pickle(fname, s_path)

Dumps the .eit information to a pickle file.

single_hex_to_int(str_num)

Delete the hexadecimal 0x python notation.

split_bursts_in_frames(split_list, cnf)

Takes the splitted list from reshape_full_message_in_bursts() and parses the single frames.

serial – serial connection

sciopy.GetLEDControl(serial, led, mode)[source]

Set a defined led to mode off, on or blink.

Parameters

  • serial – serial connection

  • led (int) – [1,2,3,4] led number

  • mode (str) – [‘enable’,’disable’, ‘blink’] mode

Return type

None

serial – serial connection

serial – serial connection

sciopy.SetBurstCount(serial, measuremen_cnf)[source]

Set the burst count to one of the provided values [1, 5, 10, 100].

Parameters

  • serial – serial connection

  • measuremen_cnf (ScioSpecMeasurementConfig) – dataclass object with configurations

Return type

None

sciopy.SetLEDControl(serial, led, mode)[source]

Disable or enable auto mode.

Parameters

  • serial – serial connection

  • led (int) – [1,2,3,4] led number

  • mode (str) – [‘enable’,’disable’] mode

Return type

None

sciopy.SetLED_Mode(serial, led, mode)[source]

Set a defined led to a mode.

Parameters

  • serial – serial connection

  • led (int) – [1,2,3,4] led number

  • mode (str) – [‘enable’,’disable’, ‘blink’] mode

Return type

None

sciopy.SetMeasurementSetup(serial, burst_count=10, frame_rate=1, exc_freq=[100, 100, 1, 0], exc_amp=0.01)[source]

Configurate and write own measurement setup. ->!!! TBD !!!<-

Parameters

  • serial – serial connection to the ScioSpecEIT device.

  • burst_count (int) – number of measurement between start and stop command.

  • frame_rate (int) – number of measurements per second.

  • exc_freq (list) – [fmin, fmax, fcount, ftype] add excitation frequency block in Hz.

  • exc_amp (float) – Set excitation amplitude in ampere.

  • information (Further) –

  • ------------------- –

  • [fmin] –

    • minimum frequency fmin

    • 4 Byte floating point single precision value

    • range = 100 Hz - 10 MHz

    • Default: fmin = 100 kHz

  • [fmax] –

    • maximum frequency fmax

    • 4 Byte floating point single precision value

    • range = 100 Hz - 10 MHz

    • Default: fmax = 100 kHz

  • [fcount] –

    • frequency count fcount

    • 2 Byte unsigned integer value

    • range = 1 - 128

    • Default: fcount = 1

  • [ftype] –

    • frequency type ftype

    • 1 Byte unsigned interger value

    • ftype = 0: linear frequency distribution | 1: logarithmic frequency distribution

    • Default: ftype = 0

  • amplitude] ([excitation) –

    • 8 Byte Floating Point double precision value.

    • Amin = 100 nA

    • Amax = 10 mA

    • Step size see Chapter “Technical Specification”

    • Default: A = 0.01 A

Return type

None

sciopy.SoftwareReset(serial)[source]

Reset the ScioSpec software.

Parameters

serial – serial connection

Return type

None

sciopy.StartStopMeasurement(serial)[source]

Start and stop the measurement and return the serial message buffer.

Parameters

serial – serial connection

Returns

message buffer

Return type

list

sciopy.SystemMessageCallback(serial, prnt_msg=True, ret_hex_int=None)[source]

Reads the message buffer of a serial connection. Also prints out the general system message.

Parameters

  • serial – serial connection

  • prnt_msg (bool) – if true print message, if false not

  • ret_hex_int (Union[None, str]) – use [‘none’,’hex’, ‘int’, ‘both’] to return nothing, hex or integer data or both.

Returns

return depens on the ret_hex_int variable

Return type

[None, received_hex, received, (received, received_hex)]

sciopy.available_serial_ports()[source]

Lists serial port names.

Returns

a list of the serial ports available on the system

Return type

list

Raises

  • EnvironmentError – on unsupported or unknown platforms

  • OtherError – when an other error

sciopy.bytesarray_to_byteslist(bytes_array)[source]

Converts a bytes array to a list of bytes.

Parameters

bytes_array (np.ndarray) – array of bytes

Returns

list of bytes

Return type

list

sciopy.bytesarray_to_float(bytes_array)[source]

Converts a bytes array to a float number.

Parameters

bytes_array (np.ndarray) – array of bytes

Returns

double precision float

Return type

float

sciopy.bytesarray_to_int(bytes_array)[source]

Converts a bytes array to int number.

Parameters

bytes_array (np.ndarray) – array of bytes

Returns

integer number

Return type

int

sciopy.configuration_01(serial, cnf)[source]

Standart SioSpce configuration template.

->!!! TBD !!!<-

Parameters

  • serial – serial connection

  • cnf (sciopy.sciopy_dataclasses.ScioSpecMeasurementConfig) –

Return type

None

sciopy.configuration_02(serial, cnf)[source]

Amplitude [mA] 1.0 Range [V] +/-5 Framerate 1 Gain 1 Burst Count 5

Min f [Hz] 1000 Max f [Hz] 1000 Steps 1 Scale LINEAR

Measure Mode Single Ended Switch Type Reed Relais

Channel Group Wizart:

Num. of Inj. 16 Chanel Group Skip 0

Parameters

cnf (sciopy.sciopy_dataclasses.ScioSpecMeasurementConfig) –

Return type

sciopy.sciopy_dataclasses.ScioSpecMeasurementConfig

sciopy.configuration_03(serial, cnf)[source]

Amplitude [mA] 1.0 Range [V] +/-1 Framerate 1 Gain 1 Burst Count 5

Min f [Hz] 10_000 Max f [Hz] 10_000 Steps 1 Scale LINEAR

Measure Mode Single Ended Switch Type Reed Relais

Channel Group Wizart:

Num. of Inj. 16 Chanel Group Skip 0

Parameters

cnf (sciopy.sciopy_dataclasses.ScioSpecMeasurementConfig) –

Return type

sciopy.sciopy_dataclasses.ScioSpecMeasurementConfig

sciopy.configuration_04(serial, cnf)[source]

Amplitude [mA] 1.0 Range [V] +/-5 Framerate 5 Gain 1 Burst Count 100

Min f [Hz] 15_000 Max f [Hz] 15_000 Steps 1 Scale LINEAR

Measure Mode Single Ended Switch Type Reed Relais

Channel Group Wizart:

Num. of Inj. 32 Chanel Group Skip 0

Parameters

cnf (sciopy.sciopy_dataclasses.ScioSpecMeasurementConfig) –

Return type

sciopy.sciopy_dataclasses.ScioSpecMeasurementConfig

sciopy.configuration_05(serial, cnf)[source]

Amplitude [mA] 1.0 Range [V] +/-10 Framerate 5 Gain 1 Burst Count 10

Min f [Hz] 10_000 Max f [Hz] 10_000 Steps 1 Scale LINEAR

Measure Mode Single Ended Switch Type Reed Relais

Channel Group Wizart:

Num. of Inj. 64 Chanel Group Skip 0

Parameters

cnf (sciopy.sciopy_dataclasses.ScioSpecMeasurementConfig) –

Return type

sciopy.sciopy_dataclasses.ScioSpecMeasurementConfig

sciopy.configure_configuration(serial, cnf)[source]

Parameters

cnf (sciopy.sciopy_dataclasses.ScioSpecMeasurementConfig) –

Return type

None

sciopy.connect_COM_port(port='COM3', baudrate=9600, timeout=1)[source]

Etablishes a serial connection to a com port.

Parameters

  • port (str = "COM3") – name of the com port

  • baudrate (int = 9600) – communication rate

  • timeout (= 1) – timeout in seconds if no connection is available

Returns

a serial connection

Return type

serial

sciopy.convert_fulldir_doteit_to_npz(lpath, spath)[source]

Converts all .eit files in a directory to .npz files in a directory spath.

Parameters

  • lpath (str) – load path

  • spath (str) – save path

Return type

None

sciopy.convert_fulldir_doteit_to_pickle(lpath, spath)[source]

Converts all .eit files in a directory to .pickle files in a directory spath.

Parameters

  • lpath (str) – load path

  • spath (str) – save path

Return type

None

Delete the hexadecimal 0x python notation.

Parameters

lst (list) – list of hexadecimals

Returns

cleared message

Return type

np.ndarray

Disconnect serial connection.

Parameters

ser – serial connection

Return type

None

Returns single object without saving anything.

Parameters

read_content (list) – the 1st param name first

Returns

class object

Return type

SingleEitFrame

Print a list of all files inside a directory.

path (str) – path to the directory

Return type

None

sciopy.list_eit_files(path)[source]

Returns a list of all .eit files in the directory path.

Parameters

path (str) – Path to the directory

Returns

list of files with .eit ending

Return type

list

Load a pickle file into a python dictionary.

Parameters

path (str) – path to the file

Returns

dicionary of the loaded pickle file

Return type

dict

Parse single data to the class SingleFrame.

lst_ele (np.ndarray) – single measurement list element

Returns

dataclass eit frame

Return type

SingleFrame

Print information regarding the acknowledge messages.

None –

Return type

None

None –

None

Parameters

None –

Return type

None

sciopy.reduce_burst_to_available_parts(brst_cnt, leq_borders=[1, 5, 10, 100])[source]

Reduces a given burst count to a set of numbers in a defined burst count border list.

Parameters

  • burst_count (int) – total number of planned measurements

  • leq_borders (list) – list of available burst configurations

  • brst_cnt (int) –

Returns

burst counts that have to be configured

Return type

list

sciopy.reduce_burst_to_less_x(brst_cnt, leq=100)[source]

Reduces a given burst count to a list of numbers smaller than a defined border.

Parameters

  • burst_count (Union[int, list]) – number of planned measurements between start and stop command

  • leq (int) – upper border of measurements between the start and stop commans

  • brst_cnt (Union[int, list]) –

Returns

burst count as number or list of numbers

Return type

Union[List[int], int]

sciopy.reshape_full_message_in_bursts(lst, cnf)[source]

Takes the full message buffer and splits this message depeding on the measurement configuration into the burst count parts.

Parameters

  • lst (list) – full message buffer

  • cnf (ScioSpecMeasurementConfig) – dataclass object configuration file

Returns

eit frame

Return type

np.ndarray

Examples

  • input: n_el=16 -> lst.shape=(44804) | n_el=32 -> lst.shape=(89604,)

  • delete acknowledgement message: lst.shape=(4480,0) | lst.shape=(89600,)

  • split this depending on burst count: split_list.shape=(5, 8960) | split_list.shape=(5, 17920)

sciopy.serial_write(ser, cmd)[source]

Write a string to a serial connection.

Parameters

  • ser – serial connection

  • cmd (str) – message to write

Return type

None

sciopy.single_eit_in_pickle(fname, s_path)[source]

Dumps the .eit information to a pickle file.

Parameters

  • fname (str) – name of the file

  • s_path (str) – save path

Return type

None

sciopy.single_hex_to_int(str_num)[source]

Delete the hexadecimal 0x python notation.

Parameters

str_num (str) – single hexadecimal string

Returns

integer number

Return type

int

sciopy.split_bursts_in_frames(split_list, cnf)[source]

Takes the splitted list from reshape_full_message_in_bursts() and parses the single frames.

Parameters

  • split_list (np.ndarray) – splitted input list

  • cnf (ScioSpecMeasurementConfig) – dataclass object configuration file

Returns

channel depending burst frames

Return type

np.ndarray
